commit.message:
W08-3-KEYSTROKE-CUTOVER: route un-consumed keystrokes through the canonical menu

Third carve of the accelerator-execution thread (design pass W08.3), on top of
the W08.1 canonical menu source (#308) and the W08.2 keycode_to_shortcut bridge +
parity guard (#309). Makes the live editor-shell keyboard path consult the
canonical menu (default_editor_menu) instead of the hardcoded EditorKeyCommand
table + an inline Ctrl+O arm. The parity guard is the safety net; behaviour is
preserved (one intentional refinement, below).

- render_path.rs: extract the per-Command dispatch from
  drain_and_route_menu_commands into a new `pub fn route_menu_command(&mut self,
  command: Command)` (match body VERBATIM — the menu path is byte-identical); the
  drain becomes a thin loop calling it. `pub` mirrors handle_key_command's
  "public so headless tests can drive it" precedent.
- lifecycle/mod.rs: rewire the window_event keyboard ladder. On each un-consumed
  KeyDown, resolve the keystroke via keycode_to_shortcut -> the resolved menu's
  command_for_shortcut and, if bound, route the SAME Command the menu bar routes
  through route_menu_command. This collapses the former EditorKeyCommand
  Save/SaveAs/Undo/Redo arm and the inline Ctrl+O arm into one menu-sourced path.
  EditorKeyCommand stays as the fallback for the execution-only time-scale binds
  (Ctrl+2/0/4, no menu home); playback (Space/Escape) and plain-R are unchanged.
  Menu resolved on-demand (the index is static — every default_editor_menu entry
  is unconditional; keydowns are human-frequency).

Behaviour-equivalence (verified): handle_key_command's Save/SaveAs/Undo/Redo arms
call the SAME handlers as the menu router's Command::Save/SaveAs/Undo/Redo
(Undo/Redo share the identical NothingToUndo/Redo swallow); Ctrl+O inline ==
menu OpenFile. Time-scale, playback, R unchanged. Sole diagnostic delta: the
Undo/Redo non-NothingTo error warn target moves from ::lifecycle to ::menu (the
extracted body keeps the menu target; only differs on a genuine bus error, which
these never produce).

One intentional behaviour refinement: the old inline arm `key == KeyO && ctrl`
ignored Shift, so Ctrl+Shift+O also triggered Open. The canonical menu binds
Ctrl+O precisely (CTRL only), so Ctrl+Shift+O is now a no-op — the
ad-hoc-keybind -> declared-accelerator correction the W08 thread exists for.
Pinned by a new test.

Tests: accelerator.rs gains menu_path_makes_ctrl_o_precise_about_shift (Ctrl+O ->
OpenFile, Ctrl+Shift+O -> None); tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs gains 4
route_menu_command parity tests (Undo/Redo/empty-stack/Save behave like the
handle_key_command path). Stale-prose sweep: accelerator.rs doc/comments
(from_key_press is now the parity anchor + time-scale executor, not the live
executor for shared binds); open_request.rs + asset_reload.rs headers (Ctrl+O is
no longer a distinct inline "keyboard axis" — it routes via the menu path).

No Cargo/edge change, no menu-definition change, no EditorKeyCommand variant
removed (W08.4). Gate: VERIFY OK 7/7 + 10/10 arch lints; editor-shell accelerator
(5) + keyboard round-trip (14) tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
